postfix_2026_07_10: SPLIT STATE. Current values from paradigm-2026-07-10-postfix.json (writing_ad.gen, n=179; live node count 179 items / 358 posts). CLOSED IN BANK: professor name prof_dr_share 1.0 / prof_literal_professor 0 (100% Dr.<Surname>), marine_biology_count 0. PROMPT FIXED BUT BANK NOT REGENERATED (do NOT read as a live regression): distinct_student_names 50 / four_pool_share 9.5% (prompt now {Claire,Paul,Andrew,Kelly}), s1_ibelieve_share 35.75% / s2_inmyopinion_share 0%, why_end_share 15.64%, prof_words_mean 77.52. These move only when the bank is regenerated against the fixed academicWriting.js.
ground_truth: data/realExam2026/writing/academicDiscussion.json (50 items; professor_question only) + scripts/research/ad_eval/prof_posts_real.json (36 FULL professor posts hand-transcribed from .codex-tmp/ocr/*写作*.txt)
measurer: scripts/research/ad_eval/measure.mjs + measure2.mjs (detectors hand-validated in validate.mjs / validate2.mjs)
notes: The structured academicDiscussion.json captured ONLY professor_question; the full professor POST (opener + two-sided framing + question) was recovered by OCR transcription (n=36) and is the source of the highest-value dimensions (opener, framing, contractions, name). The current generator was 'recalibrated 2026-05-31' on student LENGTH but NOT on opener/framing/name/stance — those three are now the dominant gaps.

dimensions.name: professor_name_format
dimensions.unit: label string shown above the post
dimensions.target: Dr. <Surname> from a tiny closed pool {Dr. Gupta, Dr. Achebe, Dr. Diaz}
dimensions.detector: regex /^Dr\. /; tally distinct surnames
examples: Dr. Gupta
Dr. Achebe
Dr. Diaz
dimensions.current: postfix prof_dr_share 1.0 (real 0.98), prof_literal_professor 0 — bank now 100% Dr.<Surname>
dimensions.gap: CLOSED. maps_to: academicWriting.js:303-305 (prompt now uses Dr.<Surname> from ONLY {Gupta ~2/3, Diaz, Achebe}) and output-format :342 name:'Dr. <Surname>'. Old name:'Professor' / '94% of real TPO' instruction replaced.

dimensions.name: prof_opener_style
dimensions.unit: opening-clause class share
dimensions.detector: regex on first clause (validate.mjs profOpener); hand-verified line-by-line against all 36
examples: We've been discussing the impact of cultural globalization on local traditions.
This week, we have been exploring the effects of globalization on local economies.
We often discuss the impact of government intervention in the economy.
dimensions.current: PROMPT FIXED, BANK PENDING REGEN. DISC_OPENING_STYLES (academicWriting.js:376-390) recalibrated so 'We've been discussing <topic>.' is now the ~61% weighted opener; the live bank still shows the old abstract-factual leads until regenerated (opener share not re-measured in the postfix snapshot).
dimensions.gap: Fix is in-prompt. The single signature opener ('We've been discussing…', real 61%) now dominates DISC_OPENING_STYLES; bank reflects it only after regeneration.

dimensions.name: prof_two_sided_framing
dimensions.unit: share of posts that lay out BOTH sides before the question
dimensions.detector: hasContrast(): 'Some <experts>' present AND ('Others'|'while others'|'On the other hand'|'However, some'|'But critics'); canonical = /Some[^.]*\.\s*Others/ or /Some.., while others/
examples: Some economists argue that globalization leads to economic growth ... Others hold that it can harm local businesses and lead to job losses ...
Some experts believe that genetics play the most significant role ... while others argue that our environment and experiences are more important.
Some sociologists argue that social norms help maintain order ... while others believe they can restrict personal freedom and creativity.
dimensions.gap: MAJOR. Real posts overwhelmingly stage a balanced two-sided debate ('Some X argue A; Others believe B') and THEN ask. Only ~29% of generated posts do; most generated posts give one-directional factual background then a question. This balanced staging is what makes the post readable as a real prompt.

dimensions.name: prof_contractions
dimensions.unit: share of posts with >=1 true contraction (we've/it's/we'll/etc., possessives excluded)

distribution.note: almost always the opener 'We've'; otherwise contraction-light

dimensions.detector: match /\b(we've|it's|i'm|don't|we're|let's|that's|we'll|you're|can't|won't|doesn't|isn't|aren't)\b/gi
examples: We've been discussing the role of literature ...
Today we'll discuss how different factors affect educational outcomes.
I'm curious to learn your thoughts.
dimensions.gap: Real reads as conversational (72% contain a contraction, driven by the 'We've been' opener). Gen is stiffer (24%). Mostly a side-effect of the opener gap — fixing D2 (opener) largely fixes this.

dimensions.name: prof_final_question_stem
dimensions.unit: question-type share (final question of post)
dimensions.detector: questionType() classifies last '?'-terminated clause; structured run uses professor_question
examples: Do you think globalization has a positive or negative impact on local economies? Why?
What do you think is the most effective role of government in the economy?
Which do you think has a greater impact on human development: nature or nurture? Why?
dimensions.current: gen reasonably spread (the generator already accepts binary/open/which/statement types via questionType param)
dimensions.gap: small — stem TYPE distribution is roughly OK. The phrasing wrapper is the issue (see prof_why_tag).

dimensions.name: prof_why_tag
dimensions.unit: share of posts ending with 'Why?'/'Why or why not?'/'Why do you think so?'/'Explain your views'
dimensions.detector: /Why(\?| or why not\?| do you think so\?)|Explain your (views|reasoning)|Give reasons/i
examples: ... on local traditions? Why?
... in shaping societal values? Why or why not?
... mostly negative? Explain your views.
dimensions.gap: STILL LOW — bank pending regeneration (the opener/frame/Why-tag prompt fixes land only on regeneration).

dimensions.name: prof_defines_key_term
dimensions.unit: share of posts that gloss/define the key term in a sentence
dimensions.detector: /refers to|are unwritten rules|, which includes|includes novels|involves the ability/i
examples: Globalization refers to businesses and economies becoming interconnected and interdependent worldwide.
Social norms are unwritten rules that dictate how individuals should act.
Emotional intelligence involves the ability to recognize, understand, and manage our own emotions ...
dimensions.gap: ~30% of real posts include a one-sentence gloss of the central term right after the opener. Generator has no such instruction; adding it (optional, ~1/3 of posts) increases mini-lecture realism.

dimensions.name: prof_post_length_words
dimensions.unit: words
dimensions.detector: whitespace word count of full professor post
dimensions.gap: Postfix prof_words_mean 77.52 (real ~65) — still ~12w long, bank pending regeneration against the tightened target (~65w, hard-max ~80w).

dimensions.name: prof_post_sentences
dimensions.unit: sentences
dimensions.detector: split on [.?!]; count clauses len>1
dimensions.gap: minor — real averages slightly more sentences (4.7 vs 4.1) because of opener + definition + two-sided framing + Why-tag. Comes for free once D2/D3/D6/D7 are fixed.

dimensions.name: students_per_item
dimensions.unit: count

distribution.note: the 1/0 cases are OCR truncations, not a real format

dimensions.detector: len(item.students)
dimensions.gap: none

dimensions.name: student_post_length_words
dimensions.unit: words
dimensions.detector: whitespace word count per student post
examples: They provide a platform for dialogue and cooperation between countries and can help mediate conflicts before they escalate.

I think nurture has a greater impact on human development. Our environment and experiences shape our beliefs, behaviors, and personalities. While genetics provide a foundation, it is our interactions and experiences that truly define who we become.
dimensions.gap: STILL OFF despite the 2026-05-31 recalibration note in the prompt. Gen students average 72 words vs real 43 — gen is ~1.7x too long, and gen MIN (45w) is above real MEDIAN (40w). The prompt text says '~40-45 words' but the live bank was generated before that and was never regenerated. Either regenerate or the instruction isn't being honored.

dimensions.name: student_post_sentences
dimensions.unit: sentences
dimensions.detector: split on [.?!]
dimensions.gap: Gen 4.2 sentences vs real 3. Real student = stance + 1 reason (+ maybe 1 elaboration). Gen pads to 4-5. Same root cause as length.

dimensions.name: student_opener
dimensions.unit: opener-class share
dimensions.detector: openerClass() — regex on first words; 'other' hand-verified to be bare thesis statements
examples: I believe living alone is beneficial because it allows people to develop greater independence ...
In my opinion, certain information should be confidential to protect competitive advantages.
Customer feedback is essential for product development. It helps companies understand customer needs ...
dimensions.gap: STILL OFF — bank pending regeneration. Postfix s1_ibelieve 35.75% (real 74%), s2_inmyopinion 0% (real 40%). Real students are formulaic (~77% 'I believe/think' + 'In my opinion'); the bank has not shifted.

dimensions.name: student_canonical_pairing
dimensions.unit: share of items where S1 opens 'I believe/think' AND S2 opens 'In my opinion'
dimensions.detector: S1 /^I (believe|think)/ AND S2 /^In my opinion/
examples: S1 Claire: "I think nurture has a greater impact ..."  /  S2 Paul: "In my opinion, nature has a more significant role."
S1 Claire: "I think corporate transparency is essential ..."  /  S2 Paul: "In my opinion, certain information should be confidential ..."
dimensions.current: ~0% (gen S2 rarely opens 'In my opinion'; opens with named-concession instead)
dimensions.gap: 38% of real items use this exact symmetrical opener pairing (S1='I believe/think', S2='In my opinion'). It is a strong authenticity tell and the generator never produces it.

dimensions.name: s2_references_s1_by_name
dimensions.unit: share of items where Student 2 names Student 1
dimensions.detector: S1 name as \bword\b in S2 text; cross-checked against ALL student names — zero hits
examples: (counter-example — what NOT to do, from the generated bank) "I hold a different view from Claire. While interactive whiteboards are useful ..."
dimensions.current: 52/144 = 36% of gen items have S2 name S1 ("I hold a different view from Claire", "While Sarah makes a good point", "different view from Mia")
dimensions.gap: PURE ARTIFACT. 2026改后 students NEVER address each other by name (0/47) — both post independent stances as if they hadn't read each other. The generator does it 36% of the time because the user-prompt has an explicit s2ReferencesS1=true branch calibrated to OLD TPO ('37% of real TPO does this'). For 2026改后 this should be forced to 0%.

dimensions.name: student_stance_contrast_type
dimensions.unit: clean opposing vs peer-directed-nuance

distribution.note: all 12 'concessions' are intra-argument hedges vs the abstract counter-view ('While transparency is important, businesses need...'), NOT acknowledgment of the other student; only 1 uses 'Although I do see...'; 0 name the peer

dimensions.detector: concessiveRe on either student; clause context hand-inspected to confirm intra-argument vs peer-directed
examples: While transparency is important, businesses need to safeguard proprietary information and strategic plans to stay competitive.
While genetics provide a foundation, it is our interactions and experiences that truly define who we become.
dimensions.current: gen 72/144 (50%) nuanced/concessive — and gen's concessions are frequently PEER-DIRECTED ('I see the point about X but...', 'While Sarah makes a good point')
dimensions.gap: Real students hold CLEAN, fully-opposed stances (74%); the minority of hedges are 'While X, Y' aimed at the abstract topic, never at the peer. Gen is 50% nuanced and routes concession at the other student. Push generator toward clean opposing stances; allow only intra-argument 'While X, Y' hedges, never peer-named ones.

dimensions.name: student_concrete_personal_example
dimensions.unit: share of student posts with a concrete/personal example
dimensions.detector: /my (cousin|uncle|parents|family|friend|hometown|old school)|For example|for instance|I remember|when I|At my/i
examples: My parents own a store and have difficulty competing with global delivery services.
I remember the time when I played volleyball in my first year of college, and it significantly helped me develop my social skills.
For example, if my friends on social media all seem to be saying the same thing, how can we be challenged to think more independently?
dimensions.current: gen 86/288 = 30% with concrete/personal example; first-person mean 2.2/post
dimensions.gap: INVERTED vs the prompt's intent. The generator prompt pushes hard for personal anecdote ('At my old school...', 'My cousin works in retail...'), producing 30%. Real 2026改后 student posts are mostly ABSTRACT principle-level reasoning (only 9% have a concrete/personal example) and are less first-person (1.3 vs 2.2 pronouns). The model-answer voice here is general/expository, not anecdotal.

dimensions.name: student_echoes_prompt_terms
dimensions.unit: share of student posts reusing >=2 of the professor's content words
dimensions.detector: share content words (len>5) shared between student post and professor_question >=2
examples: (prompt: 'corporate transparency ... confidential') -> 'I think corporate transparency is essential ... certain information should be confidential ...'
dimensions.gap: Gen echoes the prompt's exact key terms almost always (99%); real does so 69% — real students more often restate in their own words. Minor, but gen over-couples the student wording to the stem.

dimensions.name: student_length_differential
dimensions.unit: abs char diff between the two students
dimensions.detector: abs(chars(s1)-chars(s2)) bucketed
dimensions.gap: Roughly OK on mean. Real allows an occasional clearly-longer student (11% have 100+ char gap); gen caps the gap (0 items >100). Low priority. The prompt's '35/43/22' differential note is OLD-TPO; 2026改后 leans more equal (55% within 30 chars).

dimensions.name: student_name_pool
dimensions.unit: share of student names from the closed 2026改后 pool
dimensions.detector: tally student[].name; membership in {Claire,Paul,Andrew,Kelly}
examples: Claire
Paul
Andrew
Kelly
dimensions.current: postfix distinct_student_names 50 / four_pool_share 9.5% (real 100% from four) — bank pending regeneration
dimensions.gap: STILL OFF — bank pending regen. maps_to: DISC_STUDENT_NAMES (academicWriting.js:236) is NOW ['Claire','Paul','Andrew','Kelly'] (was 50 names). Fix in-prompt; the old 50-name bank persists until regenerated.

dimensions.name: course_topic_pool
dimensions.unit: course-area distribution + topic recurrence

target.topic_recurrence: real REUSES a small pool — same prompt recurs across dates

real_courses_n50.single-instance: international relations, ethics, business management, technology, advertising, social psychology, history, art history, communications, life skills

dimensions.detector: course tally (lowercased); topic clustered by content-word signature of professor_question
examples: Do you think globalization has a positive or negative impact on local economies? Why?  (recurs 4x: 3.21, 3.23, 4.1, 5.10)
What is your perspective on the impact of cultural globalization on local traditions?  (recurs 5x)
dimensions.current: postfix marine_biology_count 0 (the worst off-band course purged). Broader course sprawl (robotics, paleontology, fashion design…) not separately re-measured; likely persists until bank regenerated.
dimensions.gap: PARTIAL. (a) marine-biology leak CLOSED. (b) Course breadth + topic-recurrence not re-measured; DISC_COURSE_LIST fix takes effect on regeneration. Real re-uses a small topic pool (66% unique); gen still maximizes novelty via existingTopics dedupe.

correlations: AUTHENTICITY BUNDLE: the single strongest 'feels like 2026改后' signal is the professor-post template — name 'Dr. Gupta' + opener 'We've been discussing <topic>.' + a 'Some <experts> argue A; Others believe B.' two-sided frame + a question ending in 'Why?'. These four co-occur in the majority of real items and co-fail in the generated bank (the generator nails none of the four by default). Fixing the opener (D-prof_opener) drags contractions (D-prof_contractions) up for free because the contraction is in 'We've'.

STUDENT FORMULAICITY: real student posts cluster tightly — short (≈43w / 3 sentences), opener 'I believe/think' (S1) paired with 'In my opinion' (S2) 38% of the time, abstract reasoning (only 9% personal example), clean opposing stances, no peer naming. The generator's 'sound like a real college student with personal anecdotes and varied openers' instruction pushes the OPPOSITE direction (longer, anecdotal, varied, peer-referencing). For 2026改后 the authentic voice is a short, formulaic, expository model-answer — NOT a lively forum poster.

DIFFICULTY != LENGTH: there is no length-difficulty signal here the way there is in BS/Repeat. Item difficulty in Discussion is carried by the QUESTION FRAMING (a 'Which of these two approaches…' or 'X or Y, Why?' that forces a genuine choice between two articulated positions) and by how cleanly the two student stances oppose — not by post length. Longer posts are not 'harder'.
deferred: Per-item difficulty rating: real items carry no difficulty label and the task is human-scored on the test-taker's essay, so 'item difficulty' can't be derived from the prompt alone. Deferred — no ground-truth signal.

Exact opener frequency precision: the opener distribution (D-prof_opener) is from n=36 transcribed posts (the 36 with a recoverable full post), not all 50. The 14 earliest items (Jan/early-Feb full-test OCRs) had the post embedded in noisy full-exam dumps and were only partially recovered. Direction is unambiguous (We've-been dominates) but the exact 61%/14%/11% split is ±a few points.

Whether 'Professor' (bare) EVER appears in 2026改后: across 36 transcribed + 50 structured items it never did (always Dr. <Surname>). High confidence it should be banned, but can't prove 0% from a 50-item sample with certainty.

Student grammar-imperfection rate: the prompt allows '1 mild imperfection per student'. Real recalled posts are reconstructed/cleaned (OCR+DeepSeek), so their surface grammar is not a reliable witness to what the real on-screen posts contained. Deferred — source can't support it.
